sections.content: Let me check the current state after SPACE. The W part should be collapsed now. Let me see if the blue square token is still there.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("Player state:")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N']:
        print(f"  {n['color']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("\nBlue squares:")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 4:
        print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Count blue squares
blue_count = sum(1 for n in seg if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 4)
print(f"\nBlue square count: {blue_count}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
The W part is collapsed (4 pixels) at [[32, 20], [32, 23]]. The blue square token is still at [[29, 21], ...]. There are still 3 blue squares.
Now the player's W part is collapsed. Let me try moving UP. The player is at row 32-35, col 20-23. The left frame is at row 28-31, col 16-19.
If I move UP, the player would move to row 28-31, col 20-23. The left frame is at col 16-19, so the player wouldn't be aligned with the left frame.
But maybe the player can move UP and then LEFT to align with the left frame. Let me try UP first.
Actually, wait. The player is at row 32-35, col 20-23. The left frame is at row 28-31, col 16-19. The player is to the right and below the left frame.
If I move UP, the player would be at row 28-31, col 20-23. The left frame is at row 28-31, col 16-19. They're at the same row range but different columns.
Then if I move LEFT, the player would be at row 28-31, col 16-19, which is the same as the left frame's position. This might collect the left frame.
Let me try UP then LEFT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at row 32-35, col 20-23 (W collapsed)
# Left frame at row 28-31, col 16-19
# Move UP to row 28-31, then LEFT to col 16-19 (same as left frame)
action(['UP', 'LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
